text:
## Mono

Nu vo ada han e cuda e Tanawa Tanawa e wap pie-it nush ta Weed cada my a too Wee a Ma tu queet me quash  me quash cada e auta quan eada we how ta quan Kaden ought to quan qua e hoeta quan sa Wee e whan e cua Cada oo we qad me on ich, e Wab wut e Na did quay, e New casa e wa nu in.

I bear witness, O my God, that Thou hast created me to know Thee and to worship Thee. I testify, at this moment, to my powerlessness and to Thy might, to my poverty and to Thy wealth.

There is none other God but Thee, the Help in Peril, the Self-Subsisting.
name: Mono
notes: Theory: Mono (California indigenous) prayer (section header: Mono). Opening: "Nu vo ada han e cuda e Tanawa Tanawa e wap pie-it nush ta Weed cada my a too Wee a Ma tu queet me quash..." No English translation available. Challenge: Mono is a critically endangered California Uto-Aztecan language with minimal documentation.
